title: Sentinel: Revocation Manager with Î” bounded-freshness, status list cache, and outage policies
description: Implement the sentinel's revocation checking module that enforces Î”-bounded freshness guarantees for credential status verification. The module maintains a local cache of StatusList bitstrings (encrypted on disk + in-memory), integrates with TrustLayerClient for on-chain anchors, verifies status list signatures, and enforces the Î” (maximum staleness) parameter from TrustPolicyRegistry. When the status list is stale beyond Î”, apply the configured outage policy (fail-closed by default). Also supports emergency on-chain revocation check for high-value credentials.
objectives: Cache status lists on disk (encrypted) and in-memory, indexed by statusListId
For each check: get anchor from chain cache (TASK-042), download list if missing/hash-mismatch, verify signature + hash
Enforce Î”: if now - anchor.updatedAt > Î”_seconds, status is stale; apply configured policy
Implement background refresh scheduler: refresh at Î”/2 with per-instance jitter to avoid thundering herd
Implement emergency revoke check: query on-chain mapping for credentialIdHash for high-value credentials
Expose Prometheus metrics: revocation_checks_total, status_stale_total, cache_hit_ratio, download_latency

steps.title: Design status list cache structure
steps.detail: Class `StatusListCache` with two layers: (1) in-memory dict `{status_list_id: StatusListEntry}` where `StatusListEntry` has fields: `status_list_id: str`, `bitstring: bytes` (raw gzip-compressed bitstring), `root_hash: str` (SHA-256 of bitstring), `updated_at: float` (from anchor), `downloaded_at: float`, `signed_jwt: str` (original StatusListCredential JWT). (2) Persistent disk cache at `$SENTINEL_HOME/.sentinel/store/status_lists/{status_list_id}.bin.enc` encrypted with AES-256-GCM (SecretStorage from TASK-038). On startup: load all `.bin.enc` files into memory. Max memory: 50 status lists; LRU eviction beyond that.

steps.title: Implement status bit extraction
steps.detail: Function `get_status_bit(bitstring: bytes, index: int) -> bool`. The bitstring is base64url-decoded and gzip-decompressed. Treat result as a flat byte array where bit at `index` is byte `index // 8`, bit position `index % 8` (LSB-first as per Bitstring Status List v1.0 spec). Return True if bit is set (credential is revoked/suspended). Guard: if index >= len(decompressed_bitstring) * 8, raise `StatusListIndexOutOfRange`.

steps.title: Implement cache fetch with hash verification
steps.detail: Method `async get_verified_entry(status_list_id: str, trust_client: TrustLayerClient) -> StatusListEntry`. Steps: (1) Get anchor from `trust_client.get_status_anchor(status_list_id)` â€” contains `root_hash` and `updated_at`. (2) Check in-memory cache: if present AND `entry.root_hash == anchor.root_hash` AND age within TTL â†’ return cached. (3) On mismatch or miss: download from Discovery public endpoint `GET /status/{status_list_id}` (no auth). (4) Parse StatusListCredential JWT â€” verify JWS signature using Discovery issuer DID. (5) Compute SHA-256 of downloaded bitstring. (6) Compare with `anchor.root_hash` â€” if mismatch, raise `StatusListHashMismatch` (integrity failure). (7) Store in both memory and encrypted disk cache.

steps.title: Implement Î” enforcement
steps.detail: Method `async check(credential_jti: str, status_list_id: str, status_list_index: int) -> RevocationCheckResult`. After obtaining verified entry: compute `staleness = time.time() - anchor.updated_at`. If `staleness > delta_seconds` (from policy params): record `stale_event` metric. Apply `REVOCATION_OUTAGE_POLICY`: (a) `fail_closed`: raise `RevocationStatusStale(staleness_seconds=staleness)` â€” pipeline returns 503; (b) `degrade`: return `RevocationCheckResult(is_revoked=False, stale=True)` â€” pipeline permits but marks context; (c) `use_cache`: return result ignoring staleness. Log WARNING on every stale serve: `event=revocation_stale status_list_id={id} staleness_seconds={n} policy={policy}`.

steps.title: Implement background refresh scheduler
steps.detail: Class `StatusListRefresher` as an asyncio background task. Maintains a refresh schedule: on each successful download, schedule next refresh at `now + Î”/2 + jitter` where `jitter = random.uniform(-Î”*0.1, Î”*0.1)`. This per-instance jitter prevents all sentinels from hammering Discovery simultaneously. On refresh failure: log WARNING, increment `status_list_refresh_errors_total` metric, retry at `now + 30s` (backoff). Stop refreshing deleted/expired status lists (not in recent VC set). Track active status_list_ids observed in recent check() calls as the refresh queue.

steps.title: Implement emergency revoke check
steps.detail: Method `async emergency_check(credential_jti: str) -> bool`. Used for high-value credentials where the producer config sets `REQUIRE_EMERGENCY_CHECK=True`. Calls `trust_client` chain client to query the `StatusRegistry.isEmergencyRevoked(credentialIdHash)` mapping, where `credentialIdHash = keccak256(credential_jti)`. This is a real-time on-chain read (not cached). On chain unavailability: apply TRUST_OUTAGE_POLICY (fail-closed returns True = assume revoked). This adds ~50-100ms latency per check â€” document in config that this should only be enabled for high-value operations.

steps.title: Configuration
steps.detail: ENV vars: `REVOCATION_OUTAGE_POLICY=fail_closed|degrade|use_cache` (default: fail_closed), `STATUS_LIST_MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES=50`, `STATUS_LIST_DISK_CACHE_PATH=$SENTINEL_HOME/.sentinel/store/status_lists/`, `REQUIRE_EMERGENCY_CHECK=false`. The `delta_seconds` (Î”) value is NOT configurable per-sentinel â€” it MUST come from `TrustPolicyRegistry.revocation_delta_seconds` (TASK-015) fetched via TrustLayerClient. This ensures all sentinels use the same Î” enforced by governance.

steps.title: Metrics and tests
steps.detail: Prometheus metrics: `revocation_checks_total{result: revoked|not_revoked|stale|error}`, `status_list_cache_hits_total`, `status_list_cache_misses_total`, `status_list_staleness_seconds{id}` Gauge, `status_list_download_duration_seconds` Histogram, `emergency_checks_total{result}`. Tests: (1) not-revoked bit returns False; (2) set bit returns True (credential revoked); (3) hash mismatch raises StatusListHashMismatch; (4) staleness > Î” with fail_closed raises RevocationStatusStale; (5) staleness with degrade returns stale=True; (6) background refresh updates cache; (7) emergency check returns True for on-chain revoked. Use mock chain client and mock Discovery HTTP.

files.libs/common/revocation/cache.py: StatusListCache with memory + encrypted disk layers
files.libs/common/revocation/bitstring.py: get_status_bit() per Bitstring Status List v1.0 spec
files.tests/unit/revocation/test_manager.py: Unit tests for all revocation scenarios

domain_entities.name: StatusListEntry
domain_entities.fields: status_list_id: str, bitstring: bytes (raw), root_hash: str, updated_at: float (from chain anchor), downloaded_at: float, signed_jwt: str

domain_entities.name: RevocationCheckResult
domain_entities.fields: is_revoked: bool, stale: bool, stale_seconds: float, checked_at: float

security_constraints: StatusList JWT signature MUST be verified before trusting bitstring contents â€” prevents forged revocation lists
Root hash from on-chain anchor MUST match computed hash of downloaded bitstring â€” prevents tampered downloads
Disk cache files must have 0600 permissions â€” checked on startup, refuse to load if world-readable
Emergency check must fail-closed on chain unavailability to prevent bypass of revocation during outage
Î” parameter MUST come from governance chain â€” sentinel cannot self-configure a larger Î” to bypass staleness
acceptance_criteria: Non-revoked credential returns is_revoked=False within TTL from cache
Status list with hash mismatch raises StatusListHashMismatch â€” never trusts tampered data
Staleness beyond Î” with fail_closed raises RevocationStatusStale
Background refresher updates cache before TTL expires under normal conditions
Emergency check correctly queries on-chain status for high-value credentials
